items.title: Hong Kong's new listing pipeline undeterred by Iran war volatility
Russia and Ukraine declare competing ceasefires
items.description: Moscow's declaration of May 9 truce comes as Kyiv revels in its adversary's 'fear' of Ukrainian drones.

items.title: Why U.S. troops are stationed in Germany, and what happens if some leave
items.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.

items.title: Ukraine and Russia Declare Separate Truces
items.description: The Russian Defense Ministry threatened a "massive missile strike on the center of Kyiv" if Ukraine violated its ceasefire.

items.title: Russia offers Ukraine May 8-9 ceasefire amid WWII anniversary, but threatens major reprisals in same breath
items.description: President Putin has unilaterally declared a two-day truce amid commemorations of the end of World War II in Moscow. Ukraine's President Zelenskyy proposed his own truce starting a couple of days earlier.

items.title: Zelensky annuncia un cessate il fuoco dalla mezzanotte tra 5 e 6 maggio
items.description: 'A partire da quel momento, agiremo in modo reciproco'

items.title: Putin and Zelensky both unilaterally declare conflicting 2-day truces
items.description: Russia threatened on Monday to launch a "massive missile strike" on Kyiv if Ukraine breached a two-day truce initiated by Russian President Vladimir Putin to coincide with World War II commemorations. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky responded to Moscow's threats saying Kyiv would observe its own ceasefire betwee…

items.title: Trump threatens to pull troops from Germany. Why Europe isn’t panicking.
items.description: President Donald Trump’s attempts to punish criticism, like that from German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, seem to be delivering diminishing returns in Europe.

items.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
items.description: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

items.title: Putin's waning popularity worries Kremlin five months ahead of Russian parliamentary elections
items.description: Internet shutdowns have hastened the decline in public trust in the president, amid economic hardships that have affected citizens' daily lives and the war in Ukraine that has only kept dragging on.

items.title: ‘Project Freedom’: a grand humanitarian gesture, or a fast track to more war?
items.description: After banging his battle drum, Trump is back in Nobel peace prize mode. But the move has dramatically raised the stakes“Project Freedom” has all the trappings of a classic episode of the Trump Show, the reality series that the rest of the world does not just have to watch, but live through and survive. It has a dramati…

items.title: War And Internet Blackout Push Iran's Economy Even Further Toward The Brink
items.description: More than a million jobs have been lost since the United States and Israel attacked Iran in late February. With the US blockade showing no signs of abating and little headway made in peace talks, ordinary Iranians are paying the price.

items.title: Hunted by drones it should have seen coming, Israel now sees its Lebanon strategy at risk
items.description: Years after drone warfare transformed other battlefields, the IDF seems caught off guard by Hezbollah's effective use of first-person and fiber-optic UAVs, leaving troops in southern Lebanon exposed yet again The post Hunted by drones it should have seen coming, Israel now sees its Lebanon strategy at risk appeared fir…

items.title: Middle East: Fighting and displacement in Lebanon; call for ‘sustained access’ in Gaza
items.description: Lebanon’s fragile ceasefire is being tested by renewed violence and rising humanitarian needs amid continuing civilians displacement, services under pressure and aid cuts.

items.title: Putin’s Chornobyl: a Régime That Will Not Permit the Wound to Heal
items.description: From the 2022 Red Forest trenches to the St. Valentine’s 2025 drone strike, the Kremlin’s war is a war on the truth of 1986 – and the men who buried that truth are now being decorated for it.
